transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thanks for calling Riverbend Health Navigation, this is Ava. What's going on today?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Hi, um, I've had a really bad headache since yesterday and now some blurry vision.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'm sorry you're dealing with that. Can I get your name and date of birth to pull up your file?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Sure, it's Melissa Turner, born March 4th, 1985.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thanks, Melissa. Is the headache the worst you've ever had, or any numbness, confusion, or trouble speaking?

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: No numbness, but it's pretty severe, like an 8 out of 10, and the light really bothers me.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Given the severity plus vision changes, that combination needs evaluation today, not a routine appointment. I'd recommend going to urgent care now rather than waiting.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: Okay, should I go to the ER instead?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: If new numbness, slurred speech, or the headache suddenly worsens, go straight to the ER. Otherwise urgent care can assess and refer if needed.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: That makes sense. Is there one near me that's open now?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Yes, Cedar Grove Urgent Care on Fifth Street is open until 9pm tonight. I can send the address to your phone.

transcript.speaker: patient
transcript.text: That would be great, thank you.
